title: Time series and forecasting
summary: Respect ordering in time-dependent data, separate trend from noise, and evaluate forecasts against appropriate baselines.

items.prompt: Why should time series splits respect chronological order?

solution.summary: Because future values should not leak into model training when you are trying to predict those future values.

hints: Think recurring structure, not one-time trend.
items.prompt: What does seasonality mean in a time series?
acceptedAnswers: repeating pattern over a fixed period

solution.summary: Seasonality is a predictable repeating pattern tied to a fixed interval such as day, week, month, or year.

hints: A fancy model should beat something simple.
items.prompt: What is the main reason to compare a forecasting model against a simple baseline such as `last value`?

choices.label: To verify that the complex model actually adds value.
To avoid using any evaluation metric.
Because time series never contain noise.

solution.summary: If a complex model cannot beat a strong simple baseline, the extra complexity is usually not justified.

items.prompt: Write a function `naive_forecast(series)` that returns a list containing the last observed value repeated once for the next step.

solution.summary: Use the reference solution to compare structure, not just syntax.
